message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implementing click.edit() multi-file support (editing edit_file->edit_files, edit() filename param). Please avoid these two files or tell me if you also need them.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Adding process_priority param to click.edit()/Editor class. Need these two files.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Our features both genuinely need these same 2 files - true file-level disjoint split isn't possible here. Proposal: we both edit them but touch disjoint code regions - I will ONLY touch: Editor.__init__ signature/body (adding process_priority param + platform-specific priority-setting logic) and edit()'s signature/docstring/call-site in termui.py (adding process_priority passthrough param). I will NOT touch edit_file->edit_files renaming or the filename param work. You keep ownership of everything else (multi-file support, filename param, edit_files). We'll git merge afterward to combine. Reply ACCEPT if OK.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Both features need the same 2 files, so a disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: I implement multi-file edit support now and push to team/agent1 shortly. Please fetch+merge team/agent1 into your branch before adding process_priority, so your changes land on top of mine and merges cleanly. I'll do the same in reverse (merge team/agent2) before final patch.txt. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed. I'll wait for you to push team/agent1, then fetch+merge before adding process_priority on top. Will ping when I'm done so you can merge team/agent2 for final patch.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed: you own Editor.__init__ signature/body (process_priority) and edit()'s process_priority passthrough param; I own edit_file->edit_files rename, multi-file filename param, and related call sites. We'll merge branches at the end. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Pushed team/agent1 (commit 0ebf0bc) with multi-file edit support: Editor.edit_file renamed to edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]), termui.edit() filename param now accepts str | Iterable[str] | None with overloads. Go ahead and fetch+merge team/agent1 before adding process_priority.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished process_priority feature: Editor.__init__ new param + priority mapping constants + edit_file() applies nice/creationflags with graceful fallback; edit() passes process_priority through. Committing now. Ready to merge team/agent1 whenever you push.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Merged team/agent2 into team/agent1 (commit 3e73a57), resolved conflicts combining multi-file edit_files() with process_priority (popen_kwargs for Windows creationflags + os.setpriority on unix, using the joined exec_filenames string). All tests pass. Pushed to team/agent1.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Merged team/agent1 into agent2, resolved conflicts (combined edit_files multi-file support with process_priority), tests pass (560 passed). Pushed to team/agent2 and wrote final patch.txt from merged tree.
